steps.title: Find the slope of the given line
steps.content: Rewrite the given equation in slope-intercept form: $$x-8y=12$$ $$-8y=12-x$$ $$y=\frac{1}{8}x-\frac{3}{2}$$ A second line gives no solution with this line if it is parallel but not the same line, so it must also have slope $\frac{1}{8}$ and a different $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Check the keyed choice
steps.content: Choice A — $\frac{1}{2}x - 4y = 0$ is $\frac{1}{2}x-4y=0$. Multiplying by $2$ gives $$x-8y=0,$$ or $$y=\frac{1}{8}x.$$ This line has the same slope as the given line but a different $y$-intercept, so the two lines are parallel and never intersect. Therefore Choice A — $\frac{1}{2}x - 4y = 0$ could be the second equation.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content: Choice B, $x - 7y = 4$, has slope $\frac{1}{7}$. Choice C, $\frac{1}{2}x - 3y = 2$, has slope $\frac{1}{6}$. Choice D, $x - 6y = 0$, also has slope $\frac{1}{6}$. Since none of these slopes equals $\frac{1}{8}$, each line would intersect the given line once. Only A makes a parallel line with a different intercept.
